nodes.x: How long wilt thou forget me, O Lord? for ever? how long wilt thou hide thy face from me?
How long, Lord? wilt thou hide thyself for ever? shall thy wrath burn like fire?
O that thou wouldest hide me in the grave, that thou wouldest keep me secret, until thy wrath be past, that thou wouldest appoint me a set time, and remember me!
Wherefore dost thou forget us for ever, and forsake us so long time?
He hath said in his heart, God hath forgotten: he hideth his face; he will never see it.
How long wilt thou not depart from me, nor let me alone till I swallow down my spittle?
How long shall I take counsel in my soul, having sorrow in my heart daily? how long shall mine enemy be exalted over me?
For mine enemies speak against me; and they that lay wait for my soul take counsel together,
My soul is also sore vexed: but thou, O Lord, how long?

plaints before me, but what it rests upon my feelings : but my fiiith is unyielding, and I intend to keep it so, as much as I can ; my feelings sympathise so with the injured, that I am grieved, and distressed, and my head aches, and large drops of cold sweat sit upon my brow ; no man or woman knows anything about iny feelings, and I do not want them to know, for I calculate to kick off all from…
Take counsel together, and it shall come to naught; speak the word, and it shall not stand; for God is with us.
Therefore, follow me, and listen to the counsel which I shall give unto you.
Consider and hear me, O Lord my God: lighten mine eyes, lest I sleep the sleep of death;
For thou art my lamp, O Lord: and the Lord will lighten my darkness.
I will not give sleep to mine eyes, or slumber to mine eyelids,
All the bright lights of heaven will I make dark over thee, and set darkness upon thy land, saith the Lord God.
And when he dies he shall not sleep, that is to say in the earth, but shall be changed in the twinkling of an eye, and shall be caught up, and his rest shall be glorious.
Lest mine enemy say, I have prevailed against him; and those that trouble me rejoice when I am moved.
I was a reproach among all mine enemies, but especially among my neighbours, and a fear to mine acquaintance: they that did see me without fled from me.
Mine enemies reproach me all the day; and they that are mad against me are sworn against me.
When mine enemies are turned back, they shall fall and perish at thy presence.
Deliver me from mine enemies, O my God: defend me from them that rise up against me.
But I have trusted in thy mercy; my heart shall rejoice in thy salvation.
And when I desire to rejoice, my heart groaneth because of my sins; nevertheless, I know in whom I have trusted.
I will be glad and rejoice in thy mercy: for thou hast considered my trouble; thou hast known my soul in adversities;
For I have said, Mercy shall be built up for ever: thy faithfulness shalt thou establish in the very heavens.
I entreated thy favour with my whole heart: be merciful unto me according to thy word.
But do thou for me, O God the Lord, for thy name’s sake: because thy mercy is good, deliver thou me.
I will sing unto the Lord, because he hath dealt bountifully with me.
I will sing unto the Lord as long as I live: I will sing praise to my God while I have my being.
I will sing of mercy and judgment: unto thee, O Lord, will I sing.
Unto thee, O my strength, will I sing: for God is my defence, and the God of my mercy.
I will praise the Lord according to his righteousness: and will sing praise to the name of the Lord most high.
